meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. Learn to build a professional SaaS using Antigravity and Claude Code in this step-by-step guide for students.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' trap, stop building toy apps and deploy a production-grade SaaS starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you move from zero to a live, functional, and impressive project that demonstrates real-world engineering capability to potential employers.
content.intro:
The job market doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about what you can ship. You’re staring at a blank screen, feeling the weight of the 'no experience, no portfolio' paradox. It’s a vicious cycle: you need a job to get experience, but you need a portfolio to get the job.

But here is the secret: hiring managers aren't looking for perfection, they are looking for evidence of process. By using a robust SaaS starter kit, you bypass the boilerplate drudgery and jump straight into the high-value architectural decisions that define a senior developer. It’s time to stop studying and start building.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Architecture
steps.description: Establish the core requirements for a scalable SaaS, focusing on authentication, billing, and database schemas.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-level architectural blueprint for a SaaS starter kit including Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project structure with Next.js and Tailwind CSS'

steps.title: Automate Authentication Workflows
steps.description: Secure your app by implementing OAuth and session management, which are standard for any production SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Supabase Auth configuration script for a Next.js application including protected routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure Supabase middleware for session validation'

steps.title: Integrate Subscription Logic
steps.description: Handle the billing layer using Stripe, a critical skill that demonstrates you understand real business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler and billing portal integration for a subscription-based SaaS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement Stripe Checkout flow and success redirection'

steps.title: Build the Dashboard UI
steps.description: Create a responsive, data-driven dashboard that shows off your ability to handle complex state management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a dashboard layout with reusable components for user analytics and settings management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add Shadcn UI components for the user dashboard'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Connect your repository to Vercel and establish a CI/CD pipeline, turning your code into a live URL.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file and environment variable checklist.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify production build and deploy to Vercel'

pro_tips: Always include a 'README.md' that details the architectural challenges you faced; it's what interviewers read first.
Implement a 'Demo Mode' so recruiters can explore your app without creating a real Stripe account.
Focus on one complex feature, like real-time data syncing, rather than five simple CRUD screens.
Use TypeScript strictly; it is the industry standard for SaaS and proves you value maintainability.
Document your failures in the commit history; it shows you can debug and iterate under pressure.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, write a 'Lessons Learned' blog post detailing the specific hurdles you overcame. Add the live project link to your resume and LinkedIn profile, then begin iterating on a unique 'killer feature' to differentiate your SaaS from others.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build a portfolio that actually works. Stop waiting for permission to be a developer and start shipping code that matters. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
